perturbation.original_text: NONDISCLOSURE OF PROPRIETARY INFORMATION Each Party agrees to protect PROPRIETARY INFORMATION of the other Party, not to use proprietary information for any purpose other than stated in the PROGRAM OBJECTIVE and not to disclose PROPRIETARY INFORMATION to any third party. The Parties shall each secure 1 <PAGE> in writing a similar non-disclosure agreement from each of its employees prior to any disclosure of PROPRIETARY INFORMATION to that person. IEC shall employ in general the same standard of care as it employs with respect to its own proprietary or confidential information of like kind and character. There shall be no obligation with respect to PROPRIETARY INFORMATION which: a) is or becomes publicly available without fault of the receiving Party; b) can be shown by competent written proof to have been in the possession of the receiving Party as of the date of the disclosure; c) is disclosed without restriction to the receiving Party by a third party having the legal right to make such disclosures.
perturbation.changed_text: DEFINITIONS PROGRAM as used herein means an exploratory exchange and evaluation of technical and business information among the Parties pertaining to the EFL System. PROPRIETARY INFORMATION - any information disclosed by one PARTY to the respective other PARTY, which is transferred: (a) by means of documents including, but not limited to, agreements, drawings or pictures, designated as confidential at the time of disclosure and marked accordingly; (b) any specimen, physical item or any other object, whether it be marked "confidential" or not; (c) visual or verbal disclosure which is subsequently contained in a document marked "confidential" and delivered to the receiving PARTY within 60 days from its disclosure. NONDISCLOSURE OF PROPRIETARY INFORMATION Each Party agrees to protect PROPRIETARY INFORMATION of the other Party, not to use proprietary information for any purpose other than stated in the PROGRAM OBJECTIVE and not to disclose PROPRIETARY INFORMATION to any third party. The Parties shall each secure 1 <PAGE> in writing a similar non-disclosure agreement from each of its employees prior to any disclosure of PROPRIETARY INFORMATION to that person. IEC shall employ in general the same standard of care as it employs with respect to its own proprietary or confidential information of like kind and character. There shall be no obligation with respect to PROPRIETARY INFORMATION which: a) is or becomes publicly available without fault of the receiving Party; b) can be shown by competent written proof to have been in the possession of the receiving Party as of the date of the disclosure; c) is disclosed without restriction to the receiving Party by a third party having the legal right to make such disclosures. NOTICE Any notice required or permitted under this Agreement shall be submitted in writing, shall become effective at the time of its receipt, and shall be addressed as follows, or to such other addresses either party designates from time to time: If to EFL: Mr. Yehuda Harats Electric Fuel Ltd. Har Hotzvim, P.O. Box 23073 Jerusalem 91230 Israel Fax: 02-322-252 2 <PAGE> If to IEC: Mr. Yigal Porat Israel Electric Corporation Ltd. 2 Ha'hagana Street Haifa, Israel Fax: 04-370-690
perturbation.explanation: Moving the definition of Proprietary Information before the nondisclosure agreement moves the definition of proprietary information away from the nondisclosure terms. This makes it unclear what information is defined as proprietary and moves it far away from the non-disclosure agreement. Then moving the Notice section to the end means that there could be a dispute on how notices were sent.
perturbation.law_citation: Section 25 of the Contracts (General Part) Law, 5733-1973
perturbation.law_explanation: By Israeli contract law, it mandates that contracts be interpreted in a way that reflects the common intention of the parties. Separating the definition of Proprietary Information from the non-disclosure agreement obfuscates the intention of the parties as to which information is to be treated as such. In addition, obfuscating where Notices must be sent will create confusion.

perturbation.justification: The Structural Flaws - Legal Contradiction definition states that a legal contradiction arises when a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section, making it difficult for parties to locate or interpret. The Perturbation Explanation says that moving the definition of Proprietary Information away from the nondisclosure terms makes it unclear what information is defined as proprietary. This is a structural flaw that weakens the enforceability of the agreement. The Law Explanation also states that separating the definition of Proprietary Information from the non-disclosure agreement obfuscates the intention of the parties as to which information is to be treated as such. It also claims that obfuscating where Notices must be sent will create confusion. Therefore, the modification results in a clear non-compliance because it affects clarity and enforceability.
